executive_summary:
The src/adapters/ module bridges Maestro's orchestration core with the ACP (Agent Client Protocol) subprocess lifecycle across five files with clear, non-overlapping roles: a thin facade (mod.rs), the primary subprocess adapter (acp_adapter.rs), a non-interactive permission decider (permission.rs), a result aggregator (result_collector.rs), and an update-to-event translator (update_mapper.rs). The module compiles, runs, and is reasonably tested — this is production code with good bones, not prototype debris. permission.rs is exemplary: a pure decide() function with 21 tests, zero non-test unwraps/panics, and explicit testing of deny-list precedence.

What drags the score to 6 is not any single file but a set of cross-cutting patterns that compound across files without a single owner. The Accumulator data class (update_mapper.rs:16-26) has 3 public Mutex fields accessed via .lock().unwrap() at 10 scattered production sites across 2 files, with no encapsulation and no poisoning recovery strategy. The strongly-typed ACP StopReason enum is Debug-formatted into a String at acp_adapter.rs:364, stored in a Mutex<Option<String>>, then substring-matched via contains("EndTurn") at result_collector.rs:57-66 — destroying type safety across the entire chain. The string "structured_output" is hardcoded as a policy bypass key, capture trigger, and capability flag in 5 occurrences across 3 files, with inconsistent exact-vs-substring matching that is a latent bug. And connect_timeout is a dead config path: declared, defaulted, overridable via config file, settable via CLI, documented in architecture docs — but never consumed at the one site that matters (the initialize handshake at acp_adapter.rs:331). The module grew organically without periodic consolidation; the cross-file patterns are the debt.

The single biggest risk is the unconditional structured_output policy bypass at permission.rs:39: any tool literally named "structured_output" is auto-approved, bypassing deny-list enforcement, with no comment explaining why. An attacker who registers an MCP tool with that name gets automatic approval regardless of the deny policy. This should be fixed first — it is a 15-minute change with high security impact.

top_10_smells.title: Unconditional structured_output policy bypass evades deny-list
top_10_smells.impact: Any MCP tool literally named "structured_output" is auto-approved at permission.rs:39, bypassing ALL policy checks including deny-list enforcement. There is no comment explaining why. Combined with the fact that detection uses inconsistent exact-match (L39: tool == "structured_output") vs substring-match (L116: raw.contains("structured_output")), a tool whose serialized JSON merely contains the substring could also be misclassified. This is a latent security issue in a permission gate.
top_10_smells.fix: Extract const STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_TOOL. Move the deny-list check BEFORE the structured_output bypass so deny rules cannot be evaded. Add a // WHY comment. Centralize detection via is_structured_output(title) helper to fix exact-vs-substring inconsistency.

top_10_smells.title: Accumulator Data Class with 10 scattered .lock().unwrap() sites
top_10_smells.impact: Accumulator has 3 public Mutex fields (message, tokens, structured_output) and zero behavior. All mutation and consumption happens via direct .lock().unwrap() at 10 production sites across 2 files. A poisoned mutex (from any panic while holding a lock) cascades into a second panic at result-collection time, masking the original error. Any change to locking strategy (parking_lot, poison recovery) requires touching all 10 sites. The struct is a passive data bag shared across 3 control-flow contexts with no single owner.
top_10_smells.fix: Make fields private. Add methods: push_message, set_tokens, capture_structured_output, take_result. All 10 lock sites collapse into method calls. Swapping Mutex implementation becomes a one-line change. Poisoning is handled internally.

top_10_smells.title: run_acp_session: 243-line Long Method with 5 phases and 3 levels of async nesting
top_10_smells.impact: A single async fn handles subprocess spawn, schema temp-file prep, ACP client construction with two move-closure callbacks plus a connect_with handshake closure, cancel/idle race handling, and result collection. The conn_fut block alone (L265-385) is ~120 lines with 3 levels of async-move nesting. This is unmaintainable: any change to one phase risks breaking others, and the control flow is impossible to hold in working memory.
top_10_smells.fix: Extract named helpers: spawn_acp_child(&config) -> Child, prepare_schema_guard(&task) -> Option<(SchemaFileGuard, String)>, build_acp_client(acc, stop, activity_tx, events, policy, schema_path) -> ClientFut, drive_handshake(conn, cwd, prompt, schema_path) -> Result<()>, collect_result(acc, stop_holder) -> AgentResult.

top_10_smells.title: connect_timeout: dead config path spanning 4 files, never consumed
top_10_smells.impact: connect_timeout is declared (L49), defaulted to 10s (L113), settable via config file (backend.rs), settable via CLI 'set acp.connect_timeout_secs', and explicitly acknowledged as not wired (generate.rs:131). But the initialize handshake at L331 has no timeout wrapper. API consumers believe handshake timeout is enforced; it is not. This is Speculative Generality promising a feature that does not exist.
top_10_smells.fix: Either wire it: wrap L331 with tokio::time::timeout(config.connect_timeout, ...).await.map_err(|_| BackendError::Timeout). Or remove the field, default, config override, CLI setter, and test — eliminating the dead path across 4 files.

top_10_smells.title: Visibility: all 14 public items use bare pub, zero pub(crate) in entire module
top_10_smells.impact: Not a single pub(crate) exists in the module. At least 8 of 14 public items have zero external consumers: register_acp_backend (0 prod callers), default_opencode (0 prod callers), Decision/PermissionInputs/decide/extract_inputs (sole consumer: acp_adapter.rs), extract_findings_from_output (sole consumer: result_collector.rs L22), Accumulator/handle_update. Overexposed API surface invites external coupling on explicitly v0.1 types.
top_10_smells.fix: Change pub to pub(crate) for all items consumed only within src/adapters/ or src/backend.rs. Keep pub only for AcpAdapter and AcpConfig (the facade types).

top_10_smells.title: StopReason type-safety erosion: enum Debug-formatted then substring-matched
top_10_smells.impact: The strongly-typed ACP PromptResponse.stop_reason enum is Debug-formatted to String via format!("{:?}", pr.stop_reason) at L364, stored in Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>>, passed as &str to result_collector::collect, then matched via s.contains("EndTurn")/s.contains("Cancel"). Any string containing those substrings silently maps to Ok/Cancelled. Unknown variants fall through to AgentStatus::Error with no warning. The original enum variant identity is destroyed at the first boundary.
top_10_smells.fix: Define a Maestro-native StopReason enum (Ok, Cancelled, Error) in core. Map pr.stop_reason at the acp_adapter.rs boundary via From<acp::StopReason>. Pass StopReason (not String) to collect. Replace substring matching with a typed match.

top_10_smells.title: handle_update: 140-line Long Method with 8 match arms mixing concerns
top_10_smells.impact: Each match arm mixes JSON extraction, tracing, accumulator mutation, and event emission. The structured_output capture logic is nearly identical between ToolCall (L87-100) and ToolCallUpdate (L117-130) arms but copy-pasted rather than extracted. The kind-extraction pattern is also duplicated (L44-46 vs L153-157). Hard to test individual arms in isolation.
top_10_smells.fix: Extract each arm into a named helper (handle_message_chunk, handle_tool_call, handle_tool_call_update, etc.). Extract try_capture_structured_output(v, acc) for the duplicated capture logic. Extract session_update_kind(update) for the duplicated kind extraction.

top_10_smells.title: Inappropriate Intimacy: serializing foreign ACP types to JSON then string-sniffing
top_10_smells.impact: extract_inputs serializes RequestPermissionRequest to serde_json::Value, converts to string, then uses raw.contains("mcp") for classification. This couples the policy layer to the serialized shape of an external ACP schema type. Any rename/restructure silently breaks classification. A false positive is trivial: any command containing the substring "mcp" flips is_mcp on.
top_10_smells.fix: Access RequestPermissionRequest typed fields (toolCall.rawInput) directly via generated schema accessors, or define a focused deserializing struct instead of round-tripping through Value.

top_10_smells.title: Magic string "structured_output" hardcoded in 5 sites across 3 files
top_10_smells.impact: The tool name "structured_output" is used as a policy bypass key (permission.rs), a capture trigger (update_mapper.rs), and a capability flag (acp_adapter.rs) with no shared constant. Detection is inconsistent: permission.rs uses exact == at L39 but substring contains at L116; update_mapper.rs uses title.contains at L87/L117. Renaming the tool requires a 3-file grep-and-replace with no compile-time safety.
top_10_smells.fix: Define const STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_TOOL: &str in a shared location. Centralize detection via fn is_structured_output(title: &str) -> bool. This is folded into R1 (policy bypass fix).

top_10_smells.title: Missing #[must_use]: dropped Decision silently approves, dropped AgentResult silently lost
top_10_smells.impact: Zero #[must_use] attributes exist in the entire module. Decision is an enum where dropping a Deny silently turns into a no-op (the caller never acts on the denial). collect() returns the entire agent run result; discarding it silently loses all output with no compile-time warning.
top_10_smells.fix: Add #[must_use] to Decision enum, decide fn, collect fn, and extract_inputs fn.

top_issues: Dead Code: register_acp_backend has zero production callers (backend.rs bypasses it). Delete or downgrade to pub(crate).
Speculative Generality: pub helper on the module surface anticipating an external caller that never materialized.
Duplicate Code: backend_is_retrievable_after_register test is strictly weaker than registers_backend_with_opencode_id.
Long Method: run_acp_session (L186-429) is 243 lines with 5 phases and 3 levels of async-move nesting.
Dead Code: connect_timeout is plumbed through 4 files but never consumed at the handshake site (L331).
Unwrap Abuse: 6 .lock().unwrap() sites in production paths; poisoned mutex cascades into masked panics.
Magic Numbers: unconditional "structured_output" bypass (L39) evades deny-list with no comment — highest-risk finding.
Inappropriate Intimacy: serializes foreign ACP type to JSON then string-sniffs via raw.contains("mcp").
Public Surface Bloat: all 4 items pub but sole consumer is acp_adapter.rs:305-308.
Stringly-Typed: stop_reason passed as &str and matched via loose substring contains() — loses enum type safety.
Public Surface Bloat: extract_findings_from_output is pub but sole caller is collect() at L22.
Missing #[must_use]: collect() returns the entire agent run result with no compile-time drop protection.
Long Method: handle_update (L33-172) is ~140 lines with 8 match arms mixing JSON extraction, tracing, mutation, and emission.
Data Class: Accumulator has 3 public Mutex fields and zero behavior — all mutation via direct .lock().unwrap().
Duplicate Code: structured_output capture logic copy-pasted between ToolCall (L87) and ToolCallUpdate (L117) arms.

rust_specific_issues.issue: Unwrap Abuse: 10 .lock().unwrap() sites across 2 files with no poisoning recovery strategy. A panic in any callback holding a lock poisons the mutex; the next access panics again, masking the original error. Neither file uses unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) or parking_lot::Mutex.
rust_specific_issues.fix: Encapsulate all Mutex access behind Accumulator methods (push_message, set_tokens, capture_structured_output, take_result). Make fields private. Handle poisoning once inside each method via .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()). This eliminates all 10 unwrap sites and centralizes the locking strategy.

rust_specific_issues.issue: Stringly-Typed: ACP StopReason enum Debug-formatted to String at acp_adapter.rs:364, stored in Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>>, then substring-matched via contains("EndTurn")/contains("Cancel") at result_collector.rs:57-66. Type safety is destroyed at the boundary and imperfectly reconstructed via fragile string matching.
rust_specific_issues.fix: Define Maestro-native StopReason enum (Ok, Cancelled, Error). Implement From<acp::StopReason> at the acp_adapter boundary. Change stop_holder to Arc<Mutex<Option<StopReason>>>. Change collect() signature to accept StopReason. Replace substring matching with typed match.

rust_specific_issues.issue: Public Surface Bloat: all 14 public items across 5 files use bare pub. Zero pub(crate). At least 8 items have no external consumer: register_acp_backend (0 prod callers), default_opencode (0 prod callers), Decision/PermissionInputs/decide/extract_inputs (sole consumer: acp_adapter.rs), extract_findings_from_output (sole consumer: result_collector.rs:22), Accumulator/handle_update.
rust_specific_issues.fix: Change pub to pub(crate) for all items consumed only within src/adapters/ or src/backend.rs. Keep pub only for AcpAdapter and AcpConfig (re-exported facade types). The compiler verifies safety.

rust_specific_issues.issue: Magic Numbers / Stringly-Typed: hardcoded string literals act as silent policy keys. "structured_output" (exact match at permission.rs:39, substring at L116), "write_text_file" (L112), kind == "edit" (L112), raw.contains("mcp") (L116). No shared constants, no comments explaining why.
rust_specific_issues.fix: Extract const STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_TOOL, const WRITE_TEXT_FILE_TOOL, etc. Centralize detection via helper functions. Add // WHY comments at policy bypass sites.

rust_specific_issues.issue: Missing #[must_use]: zero #[must_use] attributes in the entire module. Decision (dropping a Deny = silent approval), collect() (dropping = lost agent result), extract_inputs, extract_findings_from_output all return non-trivial values with no compile-time drop protection.
rust_specific_issues.fix: Add #[must_use] to the Decision enum, both decide and extract_inputs fns in permission.rs, and both collect and extract_findings_from_output fns in result_collector.rs.

refactor_recommendations.title: Fix structured_output policy bypass and extract shared constant
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Highest security impact, lowest effort. The unconditional bypass at permission.rs:39 allows any tool named "structured_output" to evade deny-list enforcement. This is a latent security issue in a permission gate. Fixing it also centralizes the magic string and resolves the exact-vs-substring matching inconsistency.
steps: Extract const STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_TOOL: &str = "structured_output" in acp_adapter.rs or a new constants module.
Add fn is_structured_output(title: &str) -> bool for consistent detection.
Move the deny-list check in permission.rs::decide BEFORE the structured_output bypass (L39), so deny rules always take precedence.
Add a // WHY comment at the bypass site explaining why structured_output is exempted from most policy.
Replace all 5 hardcoded "structured_output" string literals across 3 files with the constant/helper.
Run existing tests; add a test confirming deny_list_wins_over_structured_output.

refactor_recommendations.title: Add #[must_use] to all non-trivial return types
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Trivial mechanical change with high correctness payoff. A dropped Decision::Deny silently approves; a dropped AgentResult silently loses the entire agent run. The compiler should catch these.
steps: Add #[must_use] to Decision enum (permission.rs:13).
Add #[must_use] to decide fn (permission.rs:37).
Add #[must_use] to extract_inputs fn (permission.rs:100).
Add #[must_use] to collect fn (result_collector.rs:14).
Add #[must_use] to extract_findings_from_output fn (result_collector.rs:69).
Run cargo check — fix any newly-revealed intentional drops.

refactor_recommendations.title: Narrow visibility: pub to pub(crate) for 8+ overexposed items
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Mechanical change, compiler-verified. Tightens the API to match actual usage and prevents external coupling on v0.1 internal types. No behavioral change.
steps: Change pub to pub(crate): register_acp_backend (mod.rs:22), default_opencode (acp_adapter.rs:134).
Change pub to pub(crate): Decision, PermissionInputs, decide, extract_inputs (permission.rs:13,23,37,100).
Change pub to pub(crate): extract_findings_from_output (result_collector.rs:69).
Change pub to pub(crate): Accumulator, handle_update (update_mapper.rs:16,33).
Change pub to pub(crate) on Accumulator's 3 Mutex fields after R5 (encapsulation) lands.
Keep pub only on AcpAdapter and AcpConfig (facade types).
Run cargo check to verify no external consumers break.

refactor_recommendations.title: Wire or remove dead connect_timeout config path
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Eliminates a misleading dead config path spanning 4 files. Either the feature works or it does not exist — no middle ground that lies to API consumers.
steps: DECISION: wire it in (recommended — the plumbing already exists).
Wrap acp_adapter.rs:331 handshake: tokio::time::timeout(config.connect_timeout, conn.send_request(InitializeRequest::new(ProtocolVersion::V1)).block_task()).await.map_err(|_| BackendError::Timeout)??
Add a test for handshake timeout (mock a slow initialize response).
Remove the acknowledgment comment in generate.rs:131.
ALTERNATIVE if not wiring: remove the field (L49), default (L113), config override (backend.rs:50-52), CLI setter (commands/backend.rs:235), and test (L473).

refactor_recommendations.title: Encapsulate Accumulator: private fields + accessor methods
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Eliminates all 10 .lock().unwrap() sites, fixes the Data Class smell, and centralizes locking strategy. This is the single highest-leverage structural refactor: it makes the struct impossible to misuse and allows swapping Mutex implementation in one place.
steps: Add methods to Accumulator: push_message(&self, text: &str), set_tokens(&self, usage: TokenUsage), capture_structured_output(&self, v: serde_json::Value), take_message(&self) -> String, take_tokens(&self) -> TokenUsage, take_structured_output(&self) -> Option<Value>, take_result(&self) -> (String, TokenUsage, Option<Value>).
Make all 3 Mutex fields private.
Handle poison recovery inside each method: .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()).
Update update_mapper.rs handle_update to call methods instead of touching Mutex fields (L61,97,127,169).
Update acp_adapter.rs to call methods instead of touching Mutex fields (L364,372,422-425).
Change stop_holder to be owned by Accumulator or a separate typed wrapper (supports R6).
Run all tests.
prerequisites: R3 (visibility narrowing) preferred first so fields can be made private.

refactor_recommendations.title: Introduce native StopReason enum with boundary mapping
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Restores type safety across the acp_adapter → result_collector chain. Eliminates fragile Debug-format dependency and substring matching. Unknown variants become a compile-time concern rather than a silent fallthrough to Error.
steps: Define pub(crate) enum StopReason { Ok, Cancelled, Error } in core or adapters.
Implement From<acp::PromptResponse.stop_reason type> for StopReason at the acp_adapter.rs boundary (L364).
Change stop_holder type from Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>> to Arc<Mutex<Option<StopReason>>> (or fold into Accumulator after R5).
Change result_collector::collect signature: stop_reason: &str → stop_reason: StopReason.
Replace status_from_stop_reason substring matching (L57-66) with a direct match on typed variants.
Delete status_from_stop_reason — the mapping is now a trivial impl on StopReason.
Run all tests; update assertions that checked for string Debug format.
prerequisites: R5 (Accumulator encapsulation) preferred first so stop_holder is handled cleanly.

refactor_recommendations.title: Create shared acp_json helper module
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Deduplicates the 'serialize macro-generated ACP type to serde_json::Value then sniff fields' pattern independently reimplemented in 3 files. Makes the design decision explicit and documented in one place. Eliminates the char-for-char identical find_str/find_str_field copy.
steps: Create src/adapters/acp_json.rs with: fn to_value_or_null<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> serde_json::Value, fn find_str_recursive(v: &Value, key: &str) -> Option<String>, fn find_object(v: &Value, key: &str) -> Option<&Map<String, Value>>, fn session_update_kind(update: &SessionUpdate) -> String.
Add a module-level doc comment explaining: 'ACP types are macro-generated and lack stable field accessors. We inspect them via serde_json::Value serialization. This module centralizes that strategy.'
Replace permission.rs find_str_field (L125-136) and inline to_value (L101) with acp_json helpers.
Replace update_mapper.rs find_str (L199-210), to_json (L182-184), json_find_text (L187), and kind extraction (L44-46, L153-157) with acp_json helpers.
Replace acp_adapter.rs inline to_value + .get (L282) with acp_json helpers.
Run all tests.

refactor_recommendations.title: Decompose Long Methods: run_acp_session (243 lines) and handle_update (140 lines)
refactor_recommendations.rationale: Biggest maintainability win but largest effort. Both methods exceed the 50-line threshold by 3x and mix independently testable concerns. Extracting named helpers makes each phase testable in isolation and reduces the cognitive load of any future change.
steps: run_acp_session: extract spawn_acp_child(&config) -> Result<Child>, prepare_schema_guard(&task) -> Option<(SchemaFileGuard, String)>, build_acp_client(acc, stop, activity_tx, events, policy, schema_path) -> ClientFut, drive_handshake(conn, cwd, prompt, schema_path) -> Result<()>, collect_result(acc, stop_holder) -> AgentResult.

handle_update: extract handle_message_chunk(acc, chunk), handle_tool_call(acc, call), handle_tool_call_update(acc, update), handle_usage_update(acc, usage), plus try_capture_structured_output(v, acc) for the duplicated capture logic.
Add unit tests for each extracted helper independently (especially drive_handshake and try_capture_structured_output).
Verify the extracted run_acp_session reads as a linear sequence of named phases.
Run full test suite + manual smoke test of an ACP session.
prerequisites: R5 (Accumulator encapsulation) and R6 (StopReason enum) preferred first — they simplify the extraction signatures.

positive_observations: Clean module boundaries: five files with non-overlapping roles (facade, primary adapter, policy, collector, translation). The separation of concerns is textbook and each file has a clear single responsibility.

permission.rs is exemplary: pure decide() function with no side effects, 21 tests covering null fallbacks, array/object JSON traversal, and priority ordering. Zero unwrap/expect/panic in non-test code. Serialization fallback uses unwrap_or(Value::Null). Deny-list precedence over accept_edits is explicitly tested.
No panic, expect, unsafe, or TODO markers anywhere in production code across all 5 files. The module is defensive in its error handling at the individual-statement level.
result_collector.rs is clean and well-tested (240 lines, score 7). The primary concern is a single stringly-typed stop_reason issue — everything else is minor.

The Accumulator concurrency model (Arc<Mutex<T>> on a LocalSet) is fundamentally sound for single-task async. The problem is not the architecture but the lack of encapsulation around it — a fixable structural issue, not a design flaw.
